subject: Samples
title: Demo Pack
notes: Generic sample questions demonstrating all four question types.

questions.prompt: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury orbits at an average distance of about 58 million kilometers from the Sun, making it the closest planet.

questions.prompt: True or False: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
questions.explanation: At standard atmospheric pressure (1 atmosphere or 101.325 kilopascals), pure water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit).

questions.prompt: Match each unit of measurement to what it measures.
questions.explanation: Kelvin measures temperature, Pascal measures pressure, Ampere measures electric current, and Hertz measures frequency. These are all SI (International System of Units) base or derived units.

questions.prompt: A library charges $0.25 per day for overdue books. You borrowed a book on March 1 and returned it on March 15. The loan period is 7 days. How much do you owe in late fees?
questions.explanation: The book was due on March 8 (7 days after March 1). It was returned on March 15, which is 7 days overdue. 7 days multiplied by $0.25 per day equals $1.75 in late fees.

questions.prompt: In what year did the first Moon landing occur?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969. Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the lunar surface.
